reasoning: Unsafe findings relate to upstream application security flaws, not malicious supply-chain behavior introduced in AUR packaging. PKGBUILD and patches are secure.
disagreement_analysis: qwen misinterpreted application design choices (Electron security flags, localhost endpoint) as supply-chain threats; deepseek correctly contextualized these as upstream risks.
coverage_issues: Neither model reviewed PKGBUILD build/package functions for hidden risks
false_positives: index.js: Electron security flags are upstream design choices, not AUR-introduced risks
installRemoteCollectionsDialog.js: Localhost endpoint is legitimate feature, not backdoor
learnings: Improve prompt: Emphasize distinguishing between upstream app risks vs AUR-introduced threats
Prioritize PKGBUILD functions (build/package) over application code during file selection
Flag Electron security configurations as application risks, not automatic supply-chain issues
